alignments.candidate_evidence: Eight years at Google Devices and Services with two promotions, scaling global commercial operations and transformation programs with demonstrated stakeholder management at scale
alignments.job_requirement: Build and maintain strategic relationships with key clients and serve as primary point of contact

alignments.candidate_evidence: Transformation program leadership and commercial operations scaling experience with cross-functional coordination, though payment-specific and migration execution depth are unproven
alignments.job_requirement: Lead and coordinate addition of new payment solutions and technical migrations for clients, working closely with cross-functional teams

alignments.candidate_evidence: Product operations background with experience in requirements definition, logic design, and transformation rollout
alignments.job_requirement: Gather customer feedback, document feature requests, and provide insights to product roadmap

gaps.gap: Role is Customer Success / Technical Account Management, not Strategy and Operations or Product Operations—core responsibility is relationship and technical support delivery, not strategic transformation or business operations leadership
gaps.mitigation: Candidate could transition if TAM scope includes strategic account architecture and product roadmap influence at portfolio level, but JD emphasizes reactive technical support and upselling

gaps.gap: Deep payments industry domain expertise required (3DS, SCA, AVS, MIT, API integrations, PCI-DSS, PSD2) with familiarity of front/back-end technologies—candidate background is Google Devices/Services operations without stated payments fintech exposure
gaps.mitigation: Technical knowledge is learnable; candidate would require 6–12 months of ramp-on payments compliance, regulatory landscape, and integration patterns. Crypto & iGaming vertical adds specialization need

gaps.gap: No evidence of direct TAM, customer success, or account management experience—background is internal operations and transformation rather than external client management or support delivery
gaps.mitigation: Candidate has stakeholder and communication skills but lacks proven track record in customer-facing delivery, technical troubleshooting, and external satisfaction ownership

gaps.gap: Limited direct technical depth in APIs, payment workflows, or compliance frameworks—background emphasizes requirements and design logic rather than hands-on integration debugging or technical mentoring of clients
gaps.mitigation: Can develop through hands-on TAM onboarding; would benefit from pairing with technical engineers early in role

gaps.gap: No stated crypto or iGaming vertical experience—niche specialization required for senior-level TAM in this segment
gaps.mitigation: Industry knowledge builds with time; prior fintech familiarity would accelerate, but not present

output.summary: Candidate is a Strategy and Operations / Product Operations leader with eight years at Google and strong transformation and stakeholder management credentials, but this role is Customer Success / Technical Account Management—a function family explicitly outside target scope. While the candidate could contribute in strategic account architecture or portfolio-level product feedback, the JD emphasizes reactive technical support, client relationship delivery, payment-compliance expertise, and vertical specialization (crypto & iGaming) in which the candidate has no demonstrated depth. Risk of misalignment is high.
uncertainties: Unclear whether 'Senior Specialist' TAM title at a tier-2 fintech company maps to Manager/L4 scope or is an IC specialist role; scope ambiguity affects seniority match
No information on candidate's familiarity with crypto or blockchain payment flows, which is critical for iGaming vertical
No data on candidate's hands-on API integration or technical debugging capability beyond requirements design
